tumr 0.6.1
  • Get started
    • Bayesian Hierarchical Linear Model
    • tumr data sets
    • Troubleshooting
  • Reference

Generalized Additive Mixed Model for Tumor Growth Data

Source: R/gam.R
gam.Rd

Generalized Additive Mixed Model for Tumor Growth Data

Usage

gam(
  tumr_obj = NULL,
  formula = NULL,
  data = NULL,
  id = NULL,
  time = NULL,
  measure = NULL,
  group = NULL,
  n_grid = 20,
  ...
)

Arguments

tumr_obj

A tumr object created by tumr.

formula

GAM fixed-effects formula. Defaults to log(measure + 1) ~ group + s(time, by = group).

data

A data frame of tumor growth measurements.

id

Column name of subject IDs.

time

Column name of repeated time measurements.

measure

Column name of repeated tumor measurements.

group

Column name of treatment group assignments.

n_grid

Number of time grid points for pairwise contrasts. Default 20.

...

Further arguments passed to gamm4.

Value

An object of class "gam" with components relevant_info, formula, fit (the gamm4 object), and contrast_df (pairwise group contrasts with SE and 95\

Examples

data(melanoma1)
mel1 <- tumr(melanoma1, ID, Day, Volume, Treatment)
gam(mel1)
#> $relevant_info
#> $relevant_info$ID
#> [1] "ID"
#> 
#> $relevant_info$Time
#> [1] "Day"
#> 
#> $relevant_info$Measure
#> [1] "Volume"
#> 
#> $relevant_info$Group
#> [1] "Treatment"
#> 
#> $relevant_info$ref_group
#> [1] "A"
#> 
#> 
#> $formula
#> $formula$gam
#> log(.measure + 1) ~ .group + s(.time, by = .group)
#> <environment: 0x5595f29a7498>
#> 
#> $formula$random
#> ~(.time | .id)
#> <environment: 0x5595f29a7498>
#> 
#> 
#> $fit
#> $mer
#> Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
#> REML criterion at convergence: 1545.05
#> Random effects:
#>  Groups   Name             Std.Dev. Corr  
#>  .id      (Intercept)      0.34277        
#>           .time            0.02827  -0.61 
#>  Xr.2     s(.time):.groupD 4.01739        
#>  Xr.1     s(.time):.groupC 2.02790        
#>  Xr.0     s(.time):.groupB 3.54814        
#>  Xr       s(.time):.groupA 0.55255        
#>  Residual                  0.75171        
#> Number of obs: 600, groups:  .id, 35; Xr.2, 8; Xr.1, 8; Xr.0, 8; Xr, 8
#> Fixed Effects:
#>         X(Intercept)              X.groupB              X.groupC  
#>               7.2661               -4.2228               -0.5356  
#>             X.groupD  Xs(.time):.groupAFx1  Xs(.time):.groupBFx1  
#>              -5.8523               -2.4256                0.3148  
#> Xs(.time):.groupCFx1  Xs(.time):.groupDFx1  
#>              -1.5688                0.8294  
#> 
#> $gam
#> 
#> Family: gaussian 
#> Link function: identity 
#> 
#> Formula:
#> log(.measure + 1) ~ .group + s(.time, by = .group)
#> <environment: 0x5595f29a7498>
#> 
#> Estimated degrees of freedom:
#> 1.23 5.66 3.41 5.94  total = 20.24 
#> 
#> lmer.REML score: 1545.05     
#> 
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "gamm4" "list" 
#> 
#> $contrast_df
#>           Time contrast    estimate        SE    lower.CL    upper.CL
#> 1     0.000000    A - B -0.04470028 0.2752499 -0.58419011  0.49478955
#> 2     7.210526    A - B  0.94501886 0.2118009  0.52988903  1.36014869
#> 3    14.421053    A - B  1.94690307 0.2355857  1.48515505  2.40865108
#> 4    21.631579    A - B  2.93229741 0.2855340  2.37265067  3.49194415
#> 5    28.842105    A - B  3.83174679 0.3518851  3.14205194  4.52144164
#> 6    36.052632    A - B  4.56991933 0.4304534  3.72623061  5.41360806
#> 7    43.263158    A - B  5.10848058 0.5189351  4.09136773  6.12559343
#> 8    50.473684    A - B  5.49573382 0.6136477  4.29298438  6.69848326
#> 9    57.684211    A - B  5.79729818 0.7109151  4.40390449  7.19069188
#> 10   64.894737    A - B  6.01883147 0.8109419  4.42938540  7.60827754
#> 11   72.105263    A - B  6.10341158 0.9146127  4.31077071  7.89605245
#> 12   79.315789    A - B  6.02718975 1.0218232  4.02441625  8.02996326
#> 13   86.526316    A - B  5.85520570 1.1322964  3.63590468  8.07450672
#> 14   93.736842    A - B  5.68030212 1.2459756  3.23818997  8.12241426
